description:
You have just joined a platform-engineering team and need to do a very quick
    network-readiness check before deploying a group of containerised
    microservices that have already been defined in three Compose snippets
    stored under

        /home/user/microservices/

    • auth-service.yaml  
    • billing-service.yaml  
    • notification-service.yaml  

    Your goal is to run a **small set of 3–4 standard Linux commands** to verify
    that the host machine will not experience port collisions and to produce a
    machine-readable report for the rest of the team.

    REQUIRED RESULTS
    1. Make sure the directory

           /home/user/network_diagnostics/

       exists (create it if necessary).

    2. Inside that directory create a file called

           port-map.log

       following *exactly* the format below:

       • The file is plain text, UTF-8, with **UNIX line-feeds (LF)**.  
       • First line is a header that must read *exactly*:

             service,host_port,container_port,protocol

       • Each subsequent line describes one microservice and must have the four
         comma-separated fields shown in the header, in this order:

             1. container_name taken from the Compose file  
             2. the host-side port (the number before the colon in the ports
                entry)  
             3. the container-side port (the number after the colon)  
             4. the literal string “tcp”

       • Sort the service lines **alphabetically by the container_name** so the
         final file contains **exactly four lines** total (header + three
         services).

       Example of the required structure (values will of course be different):

           service,host_port,container_port,protocol
           alpha-service,7001,7000,tcp
           beta-service,7002,7000,tcp
           gamma-service,7003,7000,tcp

    3. Before writing the log, run any diagnostic command you prefer
       (e.g. `ss -ltn`, `lsof -i`, or similar) to confirm whether each
       host-side port is already in use. You do **not** need to write the
       diagnostic output to a file, but you must still run the command so that
       anyone reviewing your terminal history sees the check was performed.

    4. Once the log file is created, display its contents in the terminal so the
       CI system’s captured stdout shows the final summary.

    CONSTRAINTS
    • Solve the task in a realistic manner using only 3–4 shell commands
      (compound commands, pipelines, or simple scripting one-liners are fine).  
    • You do **not** need root privileges. Everything must stay inside your home
      directory.  
    • Do **not** hard-code port numbers—parse them from the YAML files.  
    • Make sure the very last character in *port-map.log* is a newline; missing
      it will fail automated checks.

    The automated grader will verify only:

    • The presence of */home/user/network_diagnostics/port-map.log*.  
    • The file’s exact textual content and ordering, including the trailing
      newline.  

    No other output is strictly examined, but showing your diagnostic commands
    and the resulting log in the terminal is considered good practice.
truth:
<!--  Pre-existing directory structure  -->
    <preexisting>
        /home/user/microservices/
        ├── auth-service.yaml
        ├── billing-service.yaml
        └── notification-service.yaml
    </preexisting>

    <!--  Exact contents of the Compose snippets  -->
    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/microservices/auth-service.yaml"><![CDATA[
services:
  auth:
    container_name: auth-service
    image: auth-service:latest
    ports:
      - "8001:8000"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/microservices/billing-service.yaml"><![CDATA[
services:
  billing:
    container_name: billing-service
    image: billing-service:latest
    ports:
      - "8002:8000"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/microservices/notification-service.yaml"><![CDATA[
services:
  notification:
    container_name: notification-service
    image: notification-service:latest
    ports:
      - "8003:8000"
]]></file>
    </files>

    <!--  Directory that may or may not exist before the task starts  -->
    <dirToBeCreated>/home/user/network_diagnostics/</dirToBeCreated>

    <!--  Expected artefact generated by the agent  -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/network_diagnostics/port-map.log"><![CDATA[
service,host_port,container_port,protocol
auth-service,8001,8000,tcp
billing-service,8002,8000,tcp
notification-service,8003,8000,tcp
]]></expected_file>

    <!--  Notes for the automated checker  -->
    <validation>
        1. Confirm that /home/user/network_diagnostics/port-map.log exists.  
        2. Read the file, ensuring it has exactly the four lines shown above,
           including a final trailing newline.  
        3. Whitespace differences other than the final newline are not allowed.
    </validation>
